notable_pick_trades: Traded Adam Thielen to MIN for 2026 5th-round pick and 2027 4th-round pick. No 7th-round pick (sent conditional 7th to MIN in same deal).
roster_context: Surprising NFC South champions at 8-9, lost to Rams in Wild Card. Bryce Young took a step forward (3,011 yards, 23 TD) and is the franchise QB. Team made playoffs for the first time since 2017 but has significant roster holes, especially on defense (dead-last pass rush) and now at OT after Ikem Ekwonu's ruptured patellar tendon. Very tight cap space (~$9.5M effective) but can free up significant room via restructures and the expected A'Shawn Robinson trade/release ($10.5M savings). Dave Canales and GM Dan Morgan looking to build on playoff momentum.

needs.explanation: Starting LT Ikem Ekwonu ruptured his patellar tendon in the playoff loss to the Rams. Recovery is 6-12 months, meaning he could miss most or all of the 2026 season. He is on his $17.5M fifth-year option and extension talks are on hold. Backup Yosh Nijman (who replaced him) is an unrestricted free agent, as is Brady Christensen. The Panthers have not drafted an offensive lineman in the past two drafts. RT Taylor Moton is under contract through 2027 but carries a $21M cap hit. This is now a critical need at LT specifically.
needs.current_starter: Ikem Ekwonu (LT, injured - may miss 2026), Taylor Moton (RT, under contract)
needs.depth: Yosh Nijman (UFA), Brady Christensen (UFA), Jake Curhan (UFA). Virtually no tackle depth under contract.

needs.explanation: Starting center Cade Mays and guard Austin Corbett are both UFAs. Robert Hunt (RG, Pro Bowl 2024) is under contract but missed 15 regular-season games with a torn biceps and suffered a pectoral injury in the playoffs — his health is a concern. LG Damien Lewis is locked up. The Panthers had 10 different OL combinations in 2025 due to injuries. They need to re-sign at least one IOL free agent or add via draft for center and guard depth.
needs.current_starter: Damien Lewis (LG, under contract), Robert Hunt (RG, under contract but injury concerns), Cade Mays (C, UFA)
needs.depth: Austin Corbett (UFA), Chandler Zavala (under contract), Saahdiq Charles / Ja'Tyre Carter (futures deals). Center is a clear hole if Mays departs.

needs.explanation: Chuba Hubbard is on a long-term deal and is the lead back. However, Rico Dowdle, whose emergence was crucial to the division title run, is an unrestricted free agent. Losing Dowdle without replacement would weaken a run game that was the 11th-best in the NFL (122.4 YPG). Jonathon Brooks (2024 2nd-round pick) has been dealing with injuries. Need a complementary back behind Hubbard.
needs.depth: Rico Dowdle (UFA), Jonathon Brooks (under contract, injury-prone), Trevor Etienne (under contract). Losing Dowdle would hurt.

needs.explanation: Starting SS Nick Scott is an unrestricted free agent. Isaiah Simmons is also a UFA. FS Tre'von Moehrig is locked in on a 3-year deal, and Lathan Ransom (2025 4th-round pick) provides some depth. But the loss of Scott would leave a notable void opposite Moehrig. Demani Richardson started 4 games in the regular season but is a depth piece, not a starter.
needs.current_starter: Tre'von Moehrig (FS, under contract), Nick Scott (SS, UFA)
needs.depth: Lathan Ransom (under contract), Demani Richardson (futures deal). Need to replace Scott's starting role if he departs.

needs.explanation: Akayleb Evans re-signed on a one-year depth deal. Jaycee Horn (CB1) remains. But CB2 behind Horn is still depth-level only. Need is unchanged — still worth targeting in mid rounds of the draft.

A'Shawn Robinson has been granted permission to seek a trade and is very likely to be traded or released, saving $10.5M in cap space. If he departs, the rotation is Derrick Brown (under contract, 5 sacks), Tershawn Wharton (under contract but struggled with injuries), Bobby Brown III, and rookie Cam Jackson. The interior line wasn't great against the run per PFF. Losing Robinson without replacement would open a rotational hole.
needs.current_starter: Derrick Brown (under contract), Tershawn Wharton (under contract), A'Shawn Robinson (likely traded/cut)
needs.depth: Bobby Brown III (under contract), Cam Jackson (under contract), Jared Harrison-Hunte. Adequate if Robinson is replaced, but depth would be thin.

needs.explanation: Kenny Pickett (1yr/$7.5M) provides credible backup depth behind Bryce Young. One-year deal is a bridge, not a long-term answer — but the immediate depth need is satisfied. QB is no longer a pressing draft need in 2026.

Ja'Tavion Sanders and Tommy Tremble combined for just 3 touchdowns in 2025. Carolina's offense ranked 24th in red-zone success. Multiple mock drafts project the Panthers taking Oregon TE Kenyon Sadiq at #19 overall. The position lacks a dynamic pass-catching weapon to complement Tetairoa McMillan and help Bryce Young. A true difference-maker at TE would elevate the entire offense.
needs.depth: James Mitchell (RFA), Bryce Pierre (futures deal). Neither current starter is a high-end playmaker.

needs.explanation: Carolina ranked 31st in PFF pass-rush grade (60.5) and dead last in edge pressure rate (15.2%). The team had only 30 sacks in 2025, the third straight year near the league basement. Patrick Jones II was brought in last offseason but missed time; rookies Nic Scourton (5 sacks) and Princely Umanmielen showed promise but are still developing. DJ Wonnum is a UFA. There is no proven, consistent veteran pass rusher on this roster. Dan Morgan has spoken publicly about searching for pass-rushers (possibly plural). The combine buzz was that this need may be addressed via free agency or trade, but it remains the most critical need.
needs.current_starter: Patrick Jones II, Nic Scourton (R2 2025), Princely Umanmielen (R3 2025)
needs.depth: DJ Wonnum (UFA), Trevis Gipson (UFA), Thomas Incoom (RFA). Young rookies Scourton and Umanmielen are developmental pieces, not proven producers.

needs.explanation: Starting MLB Christian Rozeboom (team-high 122 tackles) is an unrestricted free agent. The other starter Trevin Wallace missed 5 games and the playoff game, earned poor PFF run defense grades. Krys Barnes and Claudin Cherelus are also UFAs. The position is extremely thin, and the Panthers need a true three-down middle linebacker. Morgan has spoken about this being a priority, and multiple mock drafts project LB in the first two rounds.
needs.depth: Krys Barnes (UFA), Claudin Cherelus (RFA), Jacoby Windmon (futures deal). Extremely thin after Rozeboom's departure.

scheme_fit_notes: Ejiro Evero runs a 3-4 defense with three down linemen and four linebackers. EDGE prospects must be able to play as stand-up OLB pass rushers. The scheme creates extra snaps for LBs, making a true three-down MLB especially valuable. Interior DL must be able to two-gap in the base and push the pocket. On offense, Canales/Idzik run a West Coast-influenced scheme that relies on timing, play-action, and a strong ground game — protecting Bryce Young with a solid OL is paramount. A pass-catching TE who can create mismatches in the seam would be ideal for their red-zone issues.
